text:
Once upon an evening, Mary shuffled into Mrs. Jenkins'. Their friendship felt like taking candy from a baby.

"How did you find today?" Mary asked as Mrs. Jenkins gripped her mug.

"Oh goodness. I did. I had an awful nosebleed." Mary chuckled.

That night, Mary picked up stars. Every night. She said they made sweet dreams.

Just dreams.

One morning, snowy birds sang.

"No fair!" Mary said.

They sang through icicles.

Mary chased them. They flew.

They fluttered!

One caught. Mary. Feather felt home.

She flew.

She flew fast.

She flew... alone.

Maybe love doesn?!

Maybe love...

She flutter.

The moon swallowed her.

That night.

Mary soared like bats.

She swooped.

A hawk.

She soared.

Moonlight.

Mary dreamed.

Stars.

You think?

Stars?


Just dreams...

So sleep well.

You've got your stars...

Maybe love?<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
